introduction: CKD is defined as a structural and functional abnormality of the kidneys persisting for 3 months or more and manifesting as markers of kidney damage or reduction in glomerular filtration rate (GFR). Markers of kidney damage include persistent microalbuminuria or overt albuminuria or haematuria. Structural abnormalities include abnormalities on imaging or on histology. CKD is also defined as GFR less than 60ml/min or presence of markers of kidney disease for 3 months or more. CKD is quite common in clinical practice as 12-26% of Nigerians have covert or overt CKD as revealed in community studies.

features: Younger patients may present with peri-orbital swelling early in the disease process
Swelling which may then progressively worsen over time and lead to ascites and anasarca
Hypertension develops in majority of patients with co-existing Congestive cardiac failure or cerebrovascular disease
Features of uraemia: Gastrointestinal system - nausea, vomiting, hiccups, diarrhoea, epigastric pain and rarely haematemesis
Chest: central chest pain of pericarditis or breathlessness with development of pulmonary oedema
Central nervous system: Tremors, derangement of sleep rhythm, drowsiness, seizures and stupor or coma
Haemopoietic system: Anaemia (pallor) and bleeding diathesis

investigations: Haematology including serology: Full blood count, clotting profile, serological tests for HBV, HCV and HIV

goals: Counselling to discourage use of nephrotoxins and ensure compliance
Reduction of proteinuria and ensuring maximum renoprotection
non_drug: Stage 1 – 2: Treat underlying cause and institute measures to retard progression if known
drug: Antihypertensive drugs including ACEI or ARBs
Iron supplementation: Ferrous sulphate, Ferrous gluconate
Parenteral iron: Iron sucrose, Iron dextran
Erythropoiesis Stimulating Agents (ESAs): erythropoetin alpha, erythropoetin beta, darbepoetin alfa, CERA
Phosphate binders: Sevelamer HCl, lanthanum carbonate, Calcium salts
Calcitriol or vitamin D analogs, calcimimetics
adverse_reactions_and_cautions: Avoid Hb level > 13 g/dl due to risk of haemoconcentration
Check iron stores before supplemental iron if received blood transfusions due to risk of iron overload
supportive_measures: Combination of different groups of antihypertensive drugs
Dietary phosphate intake limitation in hyperphosphataemia
Dialysis: Maintenance Haemodialysis, Continuous Ambulatory Peritoneal Dialysis

differential_diagnoses: Severe acute kidney injury with cortical necrosis
